description: Erection troubleshooting — fit-up problems, bolt tension issues, weld defects, column plumbness correction, equipment setting problems, and crane issues during picks.

holes_dont_align.causes: Fabrication tolerance stack-up, field measurement error, foundation anchor bolt pattern wrong, steel distorted during shipping.

solutions.drift_pin: Use drift pin (spud wrench) to align holes. Acceptable for minor misalignment (up to 1/16 inch).
solutions.reaming: If holes are close but not aligned, ream to next larger bolt size. Must be approved by engineer. Document reamed holes.
solutions.slotted_holes: If designed with slotted holes, use the slot for adjustment. That is their purpose.
solutions.field_modification: If misalignment exceeds acceptable limits, notify engineer. May require field-drilled holes, gusset plates, or splice plates. NEVER cut or enlarge holes without engineer approval.

member_length_wrong.too_long: Member does not fit between connections. May need to be trimmed (if connection allows) or returned to shop. Engineer approval required for any field cutting of primary members.
member_length_wrong.too_short: Gap at connection. May need fill plates or shim plates. Engineer approval required. Document all field modifications.
member_length_wrong.tolerance: AISC Code of Standard Practice: member length tolerance is +/- 1/16 inch for members up to 30 feet, +/- 1/8 inch for longer members.

fit_up_problems.camber: Beams are intentionally cambered (curved upward) to counteract deflection under load. Camber must be UP. If installed upside down (camber down), beam will deflect excessively. Check camber direction before setting.

bolt_tension_issues.TC_spline_wont_shear: Inner socket slipping on spline. Clean spline, try again. If socket is worn, replace. If spline still won't shear, bolt may be from a lot with higher tensile — run calibration test.
bolt_tension_issues.cant_achieve_turn: Bolt binding in hole — check for paint in hole, burrs, cross-threading. Excessive bolt stack grip length — bolt may be too short.
bolt_tension_issues.DTI_gap_wont_close: Not enough tension. Check that DTI is correct type for bolt size. Verify washer is right side up (bumps face bolt head or nut). May need larger wrench or impact.
bolt_tension_issues.bolts_loosening: Vibration, thermal cycling, or insufficient initial tension. Verify pretension method is achieving specification. For critical connections in vibrating equipment, consider lock nuts or thread-locking compound.

porosity.cause: Gas trapped in weld — wind blowing shielding gas away, contamination (oil, moisture, paint on base metal), gas flow too high or too low.
porosity.fix: Shield from wind. Clean base metal. Adjust gas flow (35-45 CFH for FCAW-GS).

lack_of_fusion.cause: Insufficient heat, wrong angle, travel speed too fast, arc wandering to one side of joint.
lack_of_fusion.fix: Increase amperage, correct gun angle, slow down, ensure arc is on the leading edge of the puddle.

undercut.cause: Excessive amperage, gun angle too steep, travel speed too fast on fillet welds.
undercut.fix: Reduce amperage, flatten gun angle, slow down. Minor undercut (1/32 inch) may be acceptable per AWS D1.1.

cracks.cause: Hydrogen cracking (insufficient preheat, wet electrodes), restraint cracking (too much joint restraint), lamellar tearing (through-thickness loading of plate).
cracks.severity: Cracks are NEVER acceptable in structural welds. Must be gouged out and rewelded. Investigate root cause before rewelding.

incomplete_penetration.cause: Root opening too small, root face too large, insufficient amperage, improper technique.
incomplete_penetration.fix: Back-gouge root if accessible and reweld. For CJP welds, incomplete penetration fails UT inspection.

out_of_plumb.check_method: Transit or total station sighting two perpendicular faces. Or plumb bob for short columns.

correction.base_plate_shimming: Add or remove shims under base plate to tilt column back to plumb. Only works for small corrections.
correction.turnbuckle_bracing: Temporary cable bracing with turnbuckle. Tighten turnbuckle to pull column to plumb. Hold until permanent bracing is installed.
correction.come_along: Use come-along between column and adjacent structure to pull into position.

out_of_plumb.NEVER: Never heat column to straighten (unless specifically approved by engineer with heat straightening procedure). Never cut anchor bolts to reposition.

equipment_setting.foundation_not_level: Shim under saddles or base plate. Verify with precision level. Do not force equipment onto unlevel foundation — causes stress and misalignment.
equipment_setting.anchor_bolt_pattern_wrong: If bolts are off, you may need to drill new holes and install post-installed anchors. Engineer approval required. This is a costly field fix — emphasizes importance of checking template before concrete pour.
equipment_setting.vessel_orientation_wrong: Nozzles facing wrong direction. Must rotate vessel. For large vessels, this requires re-rigging and crane. Very expensive field fix. ALWAYS verify orientation before releasing crane.

crane_issues.load_binding: Load catches on structure during pick. Stop. Evaluate. May need different rigging configuration or different crane position.
crane_issues.crane_walking: Crane on soft ground settles under load. Outrigger pads too small for soil conditions. Need larger pads or crane mats.
crane_issues.boom_deflection: At long radius, boom deflects — load swings toward crane. Account for this in rigging plan. Tag lines critical.
crane_issues.wind: Wind limit exceeded — stop operations. Secure loads. Do not attempt picks in high wind.

weather_delays.wind: Most crane operations limited to 20-30 mph. Bolt torquing unaffected. Welding may need wind screens above 5 mph (gas shielded) or 15 mph (self-shielded FCAW).
weather_delays.rain: Do not weld in rain. Steel surfaces must be dry. Bolt installation can continue if workers can safely access connections.

cold_weather.welding: Preheat required when ambient temperature below 32F. Even for thin material and A36 steel.
cold_weather.bolting: TC bolts may not shear correctly below 20F — calibrate with actual lot at ambient temperature.
cold_weather.concrete: Grout and concrete require freeze protection below 40F. Blankets, heaters, or heated enclosures.

hot_weather.grouting: Epoxy grout pot life shortens dramatically above 90F. Pour early morning. Keep materials in shade.
hot_weather.worker_safety: Heat stress monitoring. Water, rest, shade per OSHA heat illness prevention.
